title: Fresh Polish Cheese-Pull Frittata
description: A delicious, ovo-vegetarian-friendly brunch dish featuring fresh cheese-pull and castelvetrano olives.
instructions: In a small bowl, whisk together eggs, milk, and a pinch of salt.
Heat the butter in a 9-inch cast-iron skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Sprinkle the yakimono-grill cheese blend over the onion and garlic mixture.
Pour the egg mixture over the cheese and cook until the edges start to set, about 2-3 minutes.
Dollop the chopped castelvetrano olives on top of the eggs.
Sprinkle the chopped parsley over the olives.
Transfer the skillet to the yakimono-grill and cook for 8-10 minutes, or until the eggs are almost set and the cheese is melted and golden brown.
Remove the skillet from the yakimono-grill and sprinkle with kosher salt and black pepper.
Let the frittata rest for 2-3 minutes before slicing and serving.
Slice the frittata into 6-8 wedges and serve hot.
